subject: Sociology
title: International Students and Brain Drain
description: A fast breakdown of International Students and Brain Drain for builders.

hook.text: Millions cross borders for education. The best often stay abroad. Their home countries call it brain drain.

buildup.text: International students bring diversity and revenue. Top universities compete globally for the brightest minds.

discovery.text: When graduates stay in wealthy countries, developing nations lose the talent they invested in educating.

twist.text: Some call it brain circulation — graduates send money home, build networks, and sometimes return with skills.

climax.text: Education migration reveals global inequality: knowledge flows upward to wealthy nations like capital does.

quiz.question: What is brain drain?
options: Educated people leaving developing countries for wealthier ones
